intent.scientific_purpose: BBR (Bygnings- og Boligregistret) is the authoritative Danish register of buildings, residential/commercial units, floors, and technical installations. It records use classification, construction year, area, status, and spatial coordinates for every registered structure in Denmark. It is the primary source for any analysis requiring building stock characterisation, residential density, or physical property attributes.
research_question_links: What proportion of buildings in a municipality are used for residential purposes?
How is the building stock distributed by use code, construction era, or building type?
Which buildings contain residential units (enheder) of a given type?
How can BBR buildings be spatially joined to addresses (DAR) or parcels (MAT)?

provenance.licence: Institutional Datafordeler API key and register access rights

grounding_logic.layer_filter: virkningstid (validity snapshot) — returns records valid at the specified datetime

join_hints.note: BBR_Bygning.husnummer is a UUID foreign key to DAR_Husnummer
BBR_GrundJordstykke.jordstykke links to the cadastral parcel

query_contract.pagination: Relay cursor — all BBR queries return a *Connection type. Use `first` (int) and `after` (cursor string from pageInfo.endCursor). Response shape: edges { node { ... } } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor }
query_contract.filter_requirement: Every query REQUIRES either `virkningstid` or `registreringstid` (or one of: datafordelerRowId, id_lokalId). Queries without a time argument return an error (DAF-GQL-0009). For current data use `virkningstid` with an ISO 8601 datetime string.
query_contract.filter_syntax: Pass `where: { <fieldName>: { eq: "value" } }` using BBR_*FilterInput. String fields use DafStringOperationFilterInput with operators `eq` and `in`. Numeric fields use DafLongOperationFilterInput or DafIntOperationFilterInput. Do NOT pass kommunekode as a top-level query argument — it is only filterable via `where`.
query_contract.cost_limits: BBR_Bygning, BBR_Enhed, BBR_Opgang, BBR_Grund are annotated @cost(weight: "5000"). Keep `first` ≤ 200 to avoid timeout (HC0045). BBR_BBRSag, BBR_Etage etc. are weight 1000 and tolerate first ≤ 1000.
query_contract.example_query: { BBR_Bygning(first: 200, after: null, virkningstid: "2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z", where: { kommunekode: { eq: "0350" }, status: { eq: "6" } }) { edges { node { id_lokalId kommunekode byg021BygningensAnvendelse status } } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } } }

entities.label: Bygning (Building)
entities.note: Primary entity for building stock analysis. byg404Koordinat is a SpatialPointEpsg25832Type — request as byg404Koordinat { wkt }.

entities.label: Enhed (Residential/Commercial Unit)
entities.note: One building (BBR_Bygning) may contain many units (BBR_Enhed). Join via BBR_Enhed.bygning = BBR_Bygning.id_lokalId.

entities.label: Grund (Plot/Site)
entities.note: A plot may contain multiple buildings. Join via BBR_Bygning.grund = BBR_Grund.id_lokalId.

entities.label: Etage (Floor)
entities.note: Linked to BBR_Bygning via BBR_Etage.bygning UUID.

entities.label: Opgang (Entrance/Stairwell)
entities.note: Links a building entrance to DAR husnummer. Join via BBR_Opgang.husnummer = DAR_Husnummer.id_lokalId.

status.type: Bygningsstatus (same vocabulary for Bygning, Enhed, Grund, Etage, Opgang)

values.6: Bygning i brug (building in active use)

privacy_governance.access_requirements: Institutional Datafordeler API key with BBR register permissions
ethical_risks: Building use codes may lag behind actual use — recently repurposed buildings may retain an outdated byg021 code.
Residential count based on Bygning use codes misses units within mixed-use buildings — use BBR_Enhed for unit-level precision.

sanitisation_rituals: Always supply virkningstid to get a valid-time snapshot. Without it, the query fails with DAF-GQL-0009.
Filter by status = '6' unless explicitly studying demolished, condemned or historical buildings.
For residential unit counts prefer BBR_Enhed over BBR_Bygning use codes — a residential building may contain non-residential units and vice versa.
Do not pass kommunekode as a top-level query argument — it must be inside `where: { kommunekode: { eq: "..." } }`.
Keep first ≤ 200 for BBR_Bygning to avoid HC0045 timeout. Paginate with cursor.

recommended_filters: Always include virkningstid in every query.
Use where: { kommunekode: { eq: "XXXX" } } to scope to a municipality.
Combine: where: { kommunekode: { eq: "0350" }, status: { eq: "6" } }

To filter residential buildings: post-filter byg021BygningensAnvendelse code 100–199 client-side, or use where: { byg021BygningensAnvendelse: { in: ["110","120","130","140","150","160","185","190"] } }
known_pitfalls: status_ does not exist — the correct field is status (no underscore).
Relay cursor pagination only — page/pagesize arguments do not exist on any BBR query.
kommunekode is NOT a top-level query argument — must go inside where: { }.
virkningstid is required — omitting it causes a DAF-GQL-0009 error.
byg021BygningensAnvendelse is filterable via where but not directly in the query signature.
Geometry field byg404Koordinat requires sub-selection: byg404Koordinat { wkt } — selecting the field name alone fails with a missing sub-field error.
BBR_Bygning has cost weight 5000 — large pages time out. Stay at first ≤ 200.
Records with null byg021BygningensAnvendelse exist and must be handled when classifying by use.
